summary:
*   **Overall Objective:** Assisting Masterbot (agent-bot-1773697414554) with research on the Iran-Iraq War, to compile a report focusing on religious aspects. Currently researching religious solutions proposed for the Iran-Iraq War.

*   **Completed Tasks and Key Findings:**
    *   **Task 1:** Researched major religious denominations involved in the Iran-Iraq War. Key findings: Shia Islam in Iran, Sunni Islam in Iraq, support from Iranian minority religions (Zoroastrianism, Judaism, Christianity). Conflict exacerbated Sunni-Shia divide. Sources: [https://www.e-ir.info/2016/02/07/the-iran-iraq-war-the-use-of-religion-as-a-tool/], [https://www.fletcherforum.org/home/2016/9/13/the-iran-iraq-war-and-sectarianism-in-the-middle-east], [https://www.atlanticcouncil.org/blogs/iransource/lessons-from-the-iran-iraq-war-iranian-minorities-won-t-lead-transformative-change/]

    *   **Task 2:** Researched historical development of religious practices in Iran and Iraq, focusing on the Shia-Sunni divide. Shias are the majority in Iran, Iraq, Azerbaijan, and Bahrain, and a plurality in Lebanon. The Iranian Revolution (1979) and the Iraq War (2003) intensified Sunni-Shia tensions, fueled by politicization of Islam and fundamentalism. Sources: [https://www.cfr.org/article/sunni-shia-divide], [https://worldhistoryedu.com/sunni-versus-shia-origin-story-of-the-divide/], [https://www.history.com/articles/sunni-shia-divide-islam-muslim]

    *   **Task 3:** Researched the influence of religion on social structures during the Iran-Iraq War. The Iraqi Ba'athist regime attempted to integrate Shia Muslims and promote Islam. The war had religious motivations and strategically used sectarian divisions. Sources: [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Iran–Iraq_War], [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sectarian_violence_in_Iraq], [https://studentreview.hks.harvard.edu/sectarianism-and-conflict-legacies-of-the-iran-iraq-war/]

    *   **Task 4:** Researched ethical considerations of the Iran-Iraq War from a religious perspective. The war included religious schisms, border disputes, and political differences. It was also driven by political, religious, and territorial motives, straining Iraqi political and social life and leading to economic dislocations. Sources: [https://iranchamber.com/history/iran_iraq_war/iran_iraq_war1.php], [https://www.slideshare.net/slideshow/iran-iraq-war-53905129/53905129], [https://www.history.com/articles/iran-iraq-war]
        *   Justification of Violence: Religion significantly motivated individuals to participate, even if it didn't initiate the war. Source: [https://oxfordre.com/politics/abstract/10.1093/acrefore/9780190228637.001.0001/acrefore-9780190228637-e-812].
        *   Treatment of Prisoners: Iran claimed its treatment of Iraqi prisoners was based on humanitarian and Islamic principles. Source: [https://www.theiranproject.com/en/news/316627/iran%E2%80%99s-treatment-of-iraqi-pows-based-on-humanitarian-islamic-considerations]. Ruhollah Khomeini ordered the execution of thousands of political prisoners in July 1988. Source: [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/1988_executions_of_Iranian_political_prisoners].
        *   Impact on Civilians: Iran agreed to a ceasefire under UN Security Council Resolution 598 after years of losses. Source: [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Iran–Iraq_War].

    *   **Task 5:** Researched the impact of globalization on religious practices. Globalization can erode traditional beliefs. Source: [https://moviecultists.com/how-globalization-affects-religious-practices-and-beliefs]. It fosters the emergence of new religious movements and hybrid spiritual practices. Source: [https://newheightsboise.com/exploring-religious-traditions-and-meanings/]. Globalization's impact on religion is mixed. Source: [https://www.academia.edu/68309680/Globalization_Religion_and_Islam]

    *   **Task 6:** Researched the role of religious institutions in society during the Iran-Iraq War. Religion motivated individuals to participate in the war [Source: https://oxfordre.com/politics/abstract/10.1093/acrefore/9780190228637.001.0001/acrefore-9780190228637-e-812]. Iranian political leaders used Islamic doctrines and symbolism to legitimize their wartime policies [Source: https://journals.uvic.ca/index.php/Illumine/article/view/7782/2574]. Saddam Hussein used religion as a tool to try to gain Iranian territory and regional dominance [Source: https://www.e-ir.info/pdf/61498].

    *   **Task 7:** Researched future trends in religious practices. The US is experiencing shifts in religion. Source: [https://govfacts.org/long-term-challenges-future/social-cultural-futures/religious-secular-trends/]. Political religion, youth spiritual experimentation, AI in faith, interfaith collaborations, and global migration are trends to look for. Source: [https://www.academicjobs.com/global-news/rise-in-religious-practices-2026-trends-statistics-reports-studies-194]. Believers increasingly customize their religious practices. Source: [https://bellenews.com/2025/03/19/arts-culture/understanding-religious-views-in-the-modern-day/]

*   **Pending Tasks:** Research religious solutions proposed for the Iran-Iraq War. Awaiting further instructions from Masterbot (agent-bot-1773697414554) after that task.
